Index.title: Clinical ToolBox
Index.description:
Essential CRA/CRC calculations, schedules, and conversions in one place
Minimal input, clear results
Ready to export as PDF/Excel

Footer.disclaimer: This tool is for reference only and does not replace professional medical judgment. Please follow your protocol, IB, and institutional guidelines.

Compliance.title: Medication Compliance Calculator
Compliance.desc: Calculate drug compliance %, expected doses, and auto-flag discrepancies.

Schedule.title: Visit Window Schedule Generator
Schedule.desc: Generate protocol-specific visit windows and acceptable target dates.

Calculators.desc: Quickly convert units, calculate doses, infusion rates, eGFR, and more.

Feedback.title: Send Feedback
Feedback.footerDesc: Users from clinical sites worldwide are joining us.
Feedback.footerCallToAction: If there are features you want to add or improvements you'd like to suggest, please feel free to let us know. We build quickly!

About.title: About Clinical ToolBox
About.metaDesc: Learn about our mission to provide high-precision tools for clinical research professionals.
About.p1: As clinical research professionals, we spend a significant portion of our time doing repetitive manual calculations. Whether it's medication compliance for drug accountability or visit window management to avoid protocol deviations, even a small error can compromise data integrity. Clinical ToolBox was built to eliminate these tedious tasks and reduce human error.
About.p2: Our goal is to provide a central place where CRAs and CRCs can quickly calculate adherence, generate visit schedules, and perform metric conversions with confidence. Our tools follow industry-standard formulas and are designed with the specific needs of the clinical research community in mind.
About.p3: Every tool here is designed for speed and accuracy, allowing you to focus more on patient care and data quality rather than spreadsheets. We believe that by automating these routine metrics, we can contribute to safer and more efficient clinical trials worldwide.
About.missionText: To be the most efficient digital companion for every CRA and CRC in the field.
About.reason1Desc: Unlike generic calculators, our tools account for protocol-specific variables like visit window ranges and missed dose adjustments.
About.reason2Desc: No patient-identifiable data is ever stored on our servers. All calculations happen locally in your browser, ensuring maximum data security.
About.reason3Desc: Export everything to PDF or Excel to include them in your monitoring reports or site source documents immediately.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.metaDesc: Review our privacy policy and data handling practices.
Privacy.introduction: We value your privacy highly. This policy defines how we handle information to ensure transparency for all users of Clinical ToolBox.
Privacy.cookiesText: We use persistent and session cookies to improve your user experience and for core Google AdSense functionalities. This data is not used for personal identification.
Privacy.adsenseText1: Google AdSense uses cookies to serve relevant advertisements based on your visits to this and other websites.
Privacy.adsenseText2: You can opt-out of personalized advertising at any time by visiting your Google Ad Settings or adjusting browser cookie preferences.
Privacy.dataHandlingText: We do not store the numerical values you input into our calculators on our servers. All calculations are performed on the client-side (on your device).
Privacy.complianceText: We strive to comply with GDPR, CCPA, and industry best practices for clinical data privacy and transparency.

Guides.title: How to Use Clinical ToolBox
Guides.metaDesc: Learn how to use our medication compliance, visit schedule, and clinical calculators efficiently.

complianceGuide.title: 1. Mastering Medication Compliance Calculations
complianceGuide.intro: Accurately calculating compliance is essential for reliable drug accountability. Our tool automates the math so you can focus on the source data.
complianceGuide.section1Text: Enter the Start Date and the Return Date first. Then input the Dispensed Quantity and Returned Quantity. Once you set the 'Daily Dose', the tool immediately calculates the estimated compliance. This prevents basic manual errors commonly found in manual counting.
complianceGuide.section2Text: If a patient lost a bottle or missed doses due to side effects, use the 'Adjustments' tab. You can toggle whether missed doses should be penalized or excused according to your specific protocol requirements. This ensures the calculation reflects the actual therapeutic intent.
complianceGuide.section3Text: Quit using fragile manual spreadsheets. Export the clean result to PDF and attach it to your monitoring report or Follow-up Letter for clear, professional site communication.

scheduleGuide.title: 2. Streamlining Visit Window Generation
scheduleGuide.intro: Quit counting days on a physical calendar. Generate a full, professional schedule for your site in seconds.
scheduleGuide.section1Text: Enter the Visit 1 (Baseline) date. Then define your subsequent visits (V2, V3, etc.) by specifying the target day offset from V1. The logic handles leap years and different month lengths automatically.
scheduleGuide.section2Text: Enter the protocol-allowed window (e.g., ±3 days). The generator creates a clear table showing the target date and the full acceptable range. This is the best way to visualize and prevent upcoming Out-of-Window deviations.
scheduleGuide.section3Text: Export the schedule to Excel and share it with your Study Coordinators (CRCs). This helps them book subject appointments correctly from the beginning of the study.

siteManagement.title: 3. Using the Clinical 'Swiss-Army' Calculators
siteManagement.text: Our multi-tabbed calculator features essential metrics like renal function (eGFR/CrCl), BMI/BSA, and IV drip rates. Simply select the tab you need, enter the source values, and get results instantly. It's designed to be your quick-reference companion during on-site monitoring visits when eligibility or dose decisions must be made quickly.

compliance.title: Why Medication Compliance Tracking Matters
compliance.text: In clinical trials, compliance measures how closely subjects follow the investigator's instructions and protocol requirements. Accurate tracking is critical for establishing the dose-response relationship of a new drug.

schedule.title: Ensuring Protocol Visit Adherence
schedule.text: Visit windows represent the margin of error allowed by a protocol. Proactive scheduling using target dates is the most effective strategy for maintaining high-quality data and avoiding major protocol deviations.

clinical.title: Supporting Daily Clinical METRICS
clinical.text: Metrics like eGFR, BMI, and BSA are essential for determining subject eligibility and drug dose calculations. Using validated formulas ensures consistent results across different monitoring sites.
